# Public roadmap + vision refresh: the harness arc, derived from ADR 0020

## Context

Operator directive (2026-06-12, twice in the Epic #13012 planning session): the harness strategy should be durable beyond work items — *"we could also use the roadmap (rewrite) and project vision"*, and the knowledge-durability question (*"how do we ensure this knowledge does not fade away in future sessions?"*) names the public narrative layer explicitly.

The converged strategy now lives on Epic #13012 (and ADR 0020 once authored): Claude-Desktop parity as floor, the flat-peer cross-family institution as the category bet, four roadmap horizons (operate-your-fleet → first-agent-beautifully → assistant-to-institution → institution-as-a-service), continuing the v13 "Institution Release" storyline. The public surfaces (README roadmap fragments, vision/identity docs, learn/benefits, portal) still tell the pre-harness story.

## The Problem

Public-narrative drift: without a roadmap/vision refresh, every external reader (and every future agent reading identity surfaces) gets the v13-era story with no harness arc — and the strategy exists only in work-item space, which is exactly the fade-mode the operator flagged.

## The Architectural Reality

- This is **identity-class work**: governed by the `neo-identity-update` skill (ADR 0018 foundation — FACTS single-source-derive; FRAMING audience-segmented against a canonical apex; CTAs governed).
- **Derivation order is load-bearing:** ADR 0020 (#13020) is the canonical source; the public surfaces derive from it. Writing them in parallel would create two drifting strategy copies — hence the native `blocked_by` on #13020.
- Surfaces in scope (final list per the skill's surface inventory at execution): README roadmap section, vision/identity docs, `learn/benefits` where the harness arc belongs, portal/SEO surfaces the skill names as governed.

## The Fix

One PR (post-ADR): run the `neo-identity-update` protocol for the harness arc — fold the four horizons + the institution-as-product framing into the governed identity surfaces, derived from ADR 0020, with the v13 release-notes storyline as narrative continuity. No new claims beyond the guardrails (no performance claims pre-benchmark).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Public roadmap/vision surfaces carry the harness arc (four horizons, institution-as-product category framing) — derived from, and consistent with, ADR 0020.
- [ ] `neo-identity-update` skill protocol followed (surface inventory, FACTS/FRAMING/CTA discipline per ADR 0018).
- [ ] No public performance/endurance claims (benchmark guardrail honored).
- [ ] Cross-family review per identity-surface convention.

Release classification: post-release (product line).

## Related

- Parent: Epic #13012. Blocked-by: #13020 (ADR 0020 = the source). Board: Project 13.
- Storyline continuity source: `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` (the Institution Release).
